summary: Added an opt-in, account-backed server-driven movement nudge on top of the on-device inactivity reminder: signed-in health-sync users who grant push register an APNs token and upload a movement heartbeat, and a Vercel cron sends a 'time to move' push after real inactivity — once per idle window, never overnight — suppressing the local nudge so users get exactly one, and reverting to on-device if any opt-in gate drops.

changes.title: Pure server-side nudge policy
changes.description: shouldNudge decides whether one user is idle past their threshold, once per idle window (de-dup on last-nudge vs last-movement), and outside quiet hours; isQuietHour handles a window that wraps past midnight. Pure so the cron delegates the decision to it.

tests.name: shouldNudge › nudges a user idle past their threshold
shouldNudge › does not double-nudge within one idle window
shouldNudge › suppresses an idle nudge during quiet hours
isQuietHour › treats a wrapping overnight window as quiet on both sides of midnight

changes.title: account_push token store
changes.description: Per-user push row holding APNs tokens, the mirrored movement heartbeat, and the de-dup stamp — everything the cron scans. Helpers register/remove tokens idempotently, mirror movement onto an existing row only, list nudge candidates, and stamp sends.

tests.name: addPushToken › appends a new token to the existing set without touching other state
removePushToken › deletes the whole row when the last token is removed
mirrorMovement › does nothing when the user has no push row (never created one)
listNudgeCandidates › drops rows with no tokens (they can't receive a push)

changes.title: Push registration endpoint
changes.description: POST registers / DELETE deregisters this user's APNs token(s), bearer-authed via requireUser; DELETE with no token deregisters the whole user (opt-out).

tests.name: account/push handler › rejects an unauthenticated request
account/push handler › registers a valid device token
account/push handler › rejects an invalid device token

changes.title: APNs sender
changes.description: buildAps builds the alert payload; providerToken ES256-signs the APNs provider JWT; sendPush delivers over HTTP/2 (device glue).

tests.name: buildAps › builds a title/body alert payload
providerToken › produces a signed ES256 JWT with the key id in the header

changes.title: Scheduled nudge cron + heartbeat mirror
changes.description: A Vercel cron (every 20 min) scans candidates, applies shouldNudge, sends the APNs push, stamps de-dup, and prunes dead tokens; guarded by CRON_SECRET. The health endpoint mirrors the movement heartbeat onto the push row so the cron reads one table.

tests.name: cron/movement-nudge handler › rejects an unauthorized invocation when CRON_SECRET is set

changes.title: iOS push client + opt-in gating
changes.description: PushRegistrationService registers/deregisters the device token against the bearer session (no-op without one); ServerPushGate is the pure three-way opt-in policy that suppresses the local nudge when server push is active. The health heartbeat carries lastMovementAt/inactivityHours, and sign-out/health-off/delete-account deregister all tokens.

changes.title: APNs registration + capability (app target)
changes.description: App delegate requests an APNs token when opted in and forwards it to the backend; aps-environment entitlement and the remote-notification background mode enable push delivery. Device glue, not in the SPM build.
